169229.explanation: In our opinion, the most capable man or woman should always hold the portfolio(s). However, it is our strong preference that the executive board is a good and balanced reflection of our society.

169230.explanation: To save or control costs, VoorBaarn does not want to hire expensive external research agencies but first wants to obtain local expertise.

169231.explanation: If a complex issue involving large financial investments cannot be resolved within one council period and a prolonged dossier threatens to develop (e.g., the Speeldoos), the council can decide in the next municipal election to use a binding referendum.

169232.explanation: We do not consider it obligatory for the municipality to accommodate all sports facilities there. VoorBaarn believes the money can be better spent. Baarn has countless sports clubs excellently housed in other locations, which are easily accessible to our residents.

169233.explanation: This applies to children from families with financial worries, but not to all young people. Baarn has various schemes to help pay for sports and culture. VoorBaarn wishes to maintain this.

169234.explanation: We are absolutely in favor of preserving the programmed theater at Rembrandtlaan 35 so that schools and clubs retain their professional stage. No luxury new buildings, but affordable adjustments needed for better operation.

169235.explanation: The municipality must oversee sensible and healthy financial policy. But money may and should be spent if it benefits our residents.

169237.explanation: VoorBaarn loves liveliness in Baarn. We cherish organizations that organize major events from which local entrepreneurs benefit and our residents enjoy.

169238.explanation: Subsidies encourage certain local policies such as culture, sports, sustainability, or connection between residents. We ensure that important, unifying events can continue to be organized by not cutting their budgets. However, better distribution might be considered.

169239.explanation: VoorBaarn stands for a lively center. Fortunately, Baarn already buzzes with many activities, but the municipality should better promote these. VoorBaarn wants an easily findable and clear agenda for our residents.

169240.explanation: VoorBaarn deliberately chooses nature and recreation in the region. The municipality must properly highlight all the beauty Baarn has to offer tourists.

169241.explanation: For us, no green madness. We do not have the ambition for Baarn to be climate-neutral by 2035. We choose feasible solutions in the interest of our residents and nature: as many well-insulated houses as possible, less paving where possible, more water storage, and greening.

169245.explanation: We do not have the ambition for Baarn to be climate-neutral by 2030. We do not want measures for appearances, and heat extraction from the Eem has little chance of success. Our municipality will switch off gas only if necessary and as last, because you pay the bill for all sustainability efforts.

169246.explanation: We are all responsible for animal welfare! The executive board should have awareness and responsibility for animal wellbeing.

169247.explanation: VoorBaarn deliberately chooses to preserve our beautiful countryside and protect our vulnerable nature. Therefore, we oppose large-scale energy production through wind turbines or solar fields but support research into possibilities for a small modular reactor in the Amersfoort region.

169249.explanation: VoorBaarn does not want a permanent asylum seekers center. Temporary (emergency) shelter for asylum seekers at the gendarmerie site is limited to a maximum of 10 years unless serious disturbances occur again, in which case we advocate immediate closure. Integration and civic integration can be much better (distributed) in Baarn.

169250.explanation: Temporary (emergency) shelter for refugees must remain possible in Baarn. VoorBaarn thinks all refugees/status holders (men and women) should participate actively as soon as possible so they also contribute to the local society.

169252.explanation: VoorBaarn has managed to retain the library at Hoofdstraat 1. We want a lively meeting place for our residents there in the center of Baarn with multiple educational and social organizations, such as HKB, Eemland1, VVV, Music School, and others.

169253.explanation: We are absolutely in favor of preserving the current programmed theater at Rembrandtlaan 35 so that schools and clubs maintain their professional stage. A smaller theater would be a great loss for Baarn. VoorBaarn is proud of “the best small theater in the Netherlands!”

169254.explanation: The municipality must properly inform our residents about what they can or must do in a crisis. For us, this does not require handing out placemats. The municipality has various other options, such as social media, leaflets, the local newspaper, or the municipal website.

169255.explanation: CCTV surveillance should be deployable if there is reason for it and always according to applicable rules.

169257.explanation: VoorBaarn wants more enforcement officers in the shopping area, near schools, and in parks, so Baarn remains clean and livable, and everyone feels safe walking the streets (also in the evening).

169258.explanation: VoorBaarn wants more attention for youth by creating fun, small meeting places spread throughout Baarn. We support regular fris parties. But we want more: for example, a youth festival and outdoor film festivals during vacations.

169259.explanation: VoorBaarn wants more enforcement by officers with a swift-action policy. Officers should patrol more often in plain clothes and on bikes, also at year-end, so they are close by to actually act against (fireworks) nuisance.

169260.explanation: VoorBaarn mainly focuses on better use of existing, vacant office and retail buildings. The initiative lies with their owners. The municipality does not support this with subsidies but does facilitate such transformations with rapid permit issuance.

169263.explanation: In Baarn, 30% of all new homes are designated for social rental. This is very high and exceptional nationally. VoorBaarn does not want more social rental homes but especially also affordable rental and owner-occupied homes for starters.

169265.explanation: The municipality of Baarn has various funds and schemes for residents with little money to receive financial support for school supplies, sports, and culture. VoorBaarn wants the municipality to better inform our residents about these support possibilities.

169266.explanation: We find the services provided by PIT Baarn very important, especially for help with poverty and debts. VoorBaarn will always critically oversee that municipal funds are used well and actually benefit those who need it.

169267.explanation: The social workshop facility for residents of Baarn is provided by the Amfors Group. Beyond that, VoorBaarn sees an opportunity for the municipality to create a modern social workshop where local entrepreneurs themselves train and guide young people in learning a trade.

169268.explanation: VoorBaarn considers the services provided by PIT Baarn important, especially for residents experiencing financial worries. These services are centrally located in Baarn’s town hall to ensure low-threshold accessibility for all residents of Baarn.

169269.explanation: VoorBaarn wants Baarn to remain the hospitable municipality in the region for our residents and (city center) entrepreneurs, where it is easy to come by car, park close by, and park for free.

169271.explanation: VoorBaarn wants Baarn to continue being the hospitable municipality in the region, where it is easy to come by car, park close to (the market and shops), and park for free.

169272.explanation: The purchase and shared use of cars is a private matter. VoorBaarn believes the municipality should not interfere in this.

169273.explanation: In recent years, the municipality has created several charging points for electric cars. However, this has resulted in the loss of many public parking spaces, for example due to placing waste containers or new construction. Enough is enough!

169274.explanation: VoorBaarn recognizes that parking pressure is high in some neighborhoods. At the same time, we doubt whether introducing parking permits will solve this problem. VoorBaarn wants residents to be able to continue parking their cars without having to pay for it.

169275.explanation: For many, the car is an indispensable and necessary means of transport. VoorBaarn opposes patronizing measures that discourage car use. Our residents make their own choices consciously.

169276.explanation: Initiatives to drink coffee together or organize activities in a community living room should be taken by the residents themselves. The municipality can provide support if necessary.

169278.explanation: The municipality has a legal duty of care, so if a child needs help, that help must be provided. A hard stop on spending is therefore not possible, but costs cannot keep rising endlessly either. The money must therefore be spent differently and smarter.

169279.explanation: VoorBaarn wants more fun activities for young people. We support regular fresh parties and the youth festival, but also consider an open-air cinema during the holidays a good addition. VoorBaarn especially wants young people to organize activities themselves.

169284.explanation: The municipality is responsible for proper and timely maintenance of our public space. Initiatives from residents themselves to maintain or beautify public spaces should certainly be supported by the municipality.

169285.explanation: VoorBaarn supports initiatives that strengthen social cohesion, but neighborhood coaches should not become a new bureaucratic layer. We prefer practical 'connectors' who stand alongside residents and help with their own neighborhood initiatives. The control should lie with the neighborhood, not the municipality.

169287.explanation: VoorBaarn has wanted for years that Baarn housing seekers receive as much priority as possible.

169288.explanation: Every Baarn resident, from Baarn, Eembrugge or Lage Vuursche, has the right to mobility. But we will not pay for empty buses. VoorBaarn chooses smart, small-scale on-demand transport, preferably with local volunteers and existing providers. This way we keep Baarn accessible and affordable.

169229.explanation: The VVD chooses quality, expertise, and experience. The composition of the council must be based on suitability, not on fixed quotas or distinctions.

169230.explanation: The VVD wants to stop costly studies that yield little and focus on decisiveness and implementation instead of endless analyses.

169231.explanation: Residents must be seriously involved through real participation, but decision-making should not be unnecessarily delayed by referenda.

169232.explanation: The VVD supports a broad sports corridor along the Geerenweg with space for multiple sports, suited to growth, with more facilities for young people and livability.

169233.explanation: Sport and culture must be accessible, but the VVD is cautious with new funds and prefers targeted and responsible support.

169234.explanation: For the Speeldoos, swift action is needed! The VVD supports the continuation of the Speeldoos and endorses a renovation, provided it is financially feasible and carefully executed.

169235.explanation: The general reserve is a buffer for setbacks. The VVD does not want to cover structural deficits with savings and always advocates careful use of residents’ tax money.

169237.explanation: Events contribute to liveliness and the economy. Standard extra taxes harm initiative and entrepreneurship. The VVD prefers appropriate arrangements in this area.

169238.explanation: Subsidies must demonstrably contribute to improvement for residents. The VVD wants to stop subsidies without clear results and always advocates careful use of residents’ tax money.

169239.explanation: A lively city center strengthens the economy and attractiveness. Events help with this, in cooperation with entrepreneurs.

169240.explanation: Baarn has unique qualities. Responsible tourism strengthens the local economy and amenities.

169241.explanation: The VVD takes responsibility for sustainability but rejects strict deadlines without realism, affordability, and freedom of choice.

169245.explanation: The VVD is open to innovation, but only if it is feasible, affordable, and responsible, and when there is room for initiatives from citizens and the private sector.

169246.explanation: Animal welfare is important but does not justify a separate portfolio. Governance must remain efficient.

169247.explanation: The VVD rejects wind turbines around Baarn and in nature reserves; preserving our beautiful public space is paramount. However, the VVD sees opportunities for private initiatives for solar panels and smart innovations.

169249.explanation: Large-scale reception centers do not work. The VVD also clearly and firmly favors limiting the influx of asylum seekers.

169250.explanation: The VVD clearly and firmly chooses to limit the influx of asylum seekers as much as possible.

169252.explanation: The VVD supports education and wants library facilities to be future-proof and used effectively.

169253.explanation: VVD Baarn wants to preserve the Speeldoos and renew it carefully as soon as possible. New large-scale investments in culture require restraint: choices must be financially feasible, broadly supported, and efficient in using public funds.

169254.explanation: Symbolic measures without demonstrable effect do not fit no-nonsense policy. VVD Baarn chooses effective and tangible solutions that genuinely contribute to safety and resilience.

169255.explanation: For VVD Baarn, enforcement is paramount. Camera surveillance is an effective means to tackle nuisance and crime. In its deployment, we deliberately choose camera technology purchased within Europe, with attention to security and privacy.

169257.explanation: Enforcement is a top priority for the VVD. More visible enforcement officers (boa’s) are necessary.

169258.explanation: The VVD chooses professional youth work and good facilities, not automatically hangout spots. In addition, enforcement is a top priority for the VVD.

169259.explanation: Nuisance and insecurity require consistent enforcement. Enforcement is a top priority for the VVD. More visible enforcement officers (boa’s) are necessary.

169260.explanation: VVD Baarn sees opportunities for housing development through transformation but mainly supports private initiatives. The focus is on accelerating permit procedures and removing obstacles, not on providing subsidies as a standard.

169263.explanation: The VVD rejects rigid quotas and wants construction in all categories to ensure healthy flow. The primary focus is on stimulating private initiatives and accelerating permit processes. Quality comes first.

169265.explanation: The VVD supports those in need but prefers targeted assistance instead of generic expansion.

169266.explanation: PIT Baarn provides support for residents and must also operate efficiently and effectively itself, so that every euro is spent as well as possible.

169267.explanation: The VVD focuses on entrepreneurship and private initiatives in the field of employment. Investments in institutions are not a government task, especially in a tight labor market.

169268.explanation: Help must be low-threshold and accessible but can also be organized centrally.

169269.explanation: We are against the introduction of paid parking: this way we do not unnecessarily burden residents and visitors with costs, entrepreneurs do not suffer economic damage, and the center and other amenities remain accessible to visitors and entrepreneurs.

169271.explanation: Accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs must be maintained. The VVD advocates for redesign and refurbishment of the Brink.

169272.explanation: Mobility choices are entirely up to the residents; the VVD does not want subsidies for behavioral steering. We do encourage entrepreneurship in this area.

169273.explanation: Charging stations are desirable, but only if there is a need for them.

169274.explanation: We are against the introduction of permit parking. Permit parking limits the freedom of choice and accessibility in Baarn. We focus on an efficient government and oppose unnecessary costs.

169275.explanation: All forms of transport are equal and residents should be free to choose which form of transport they use. This works well together in Baarn.

169276.explanation: Initiatives must come from residents, not top-down from the municipality. We focus on an efficient government and are against waste of taxpayers' money.

169278.explanation: Costs must be manageable, but adequate care remains paramount. We always strive for an efficient government and oppose waste of taxpayers' money.

169279.explanation: Good youth work prevents nuisance and strengthens talent development. The needs of young people must be leading in this.

169284.explanation: Initiatives are welcome, but maintenance remains primarily a municipal responsibility.

169285.explanation: Effectiveness is paramount; no automatic approval for new functions. The VVD primarily focuses on enforcement. We aim for an efficient government and oppose waste of taxpayers' money.

169287.explanation: The VVD wants to give young people from Baarn opportunities, within legal frameworks.

169288.explanation: Accessible public transport, such as regional taxis, increases mobility and ensures that residents can participate.

169229.explanation: A diverse executive board - not only regarding gender - is our preference. With a good reflection of society, you can better empathize with your residents. This benefits decision-making and representation.

169230.explanation: If certain expertise is necessary for good execution and is not present within our organization or among partners, it is useful to seek advice. This results in better-informed actions. We do not subscribe to the notion that investigations are a waste of money.

169231.explanation: We believe it is important that residents can voice their opinions on major or complex issues. Rather than a referendum with only 'for' or 'against' options, we prefer to engage in dialogue through, for example, a citizens' assembly or other forms of citizen participation.

169232.explanation: We want to encourage participation in sports. Sports are good for health and social interaction. By expanding the sports facilities along the Geerenweg, space is created for more and smaller sports. Accessible, affordable sports facilities are for all residents.

169233.explanation: Young people must have access to sports and culture, regardless of their parents' financial situation or other limitations. This enriches life and strengthens social contact. A fund is one way to encourage access. Even more important is preventing people from falling into poverty.

169234.explanation: The programmed theater contributes to cultural experience and social interaction. We preferred a broad cultural center in the city center, but it proved unfeasible. To break the deadlock, we want to renew the building and develop it into a place where everyone feels at home, especially youth and local associations.

169235.explanation: We stay away from the amount in the general reserve intended to cover risks. The amount that objectively does not belong there we want to use for incidental coverage of investments.

169237.explanation: If a large commercial event causes pressure on our nature, traffic, or enforcement officers, we consider it normal that the organizer contributes to those costs. This already happens: we use different rates for permits. We consider that a good system.

169238.explanation: Subsidies enable valuable initiatives by residents. They ensure that sports, culture, and welfare are accessible and affordable for residents of Baarn and stimulate interaction between residents. Fewer subsidies mean fewer activities and a less lively Baarn.

169239.explanation: Events contribute to a lively center and the use of the Brink as a meeting place. Events must consider nature, tranquility, and livability for residents. Small-scale, affordable, and accessible events are a good way to make the center more attractive.

169240.explanation: We are concerned about the pressure on our rural area from visitors who do not always follow the rules and often arrive by car. Tourists should of course enjoy our beautiful heritage and central amenities. Targeted policy must ensure a good balance.

169241.explanation: The climate crisis threatens our future and that of our children. Baarn must take responsibility in achieving climate objectives. A strict deadline like 2035 creates focus, acceleration, and concrete choices in the energy transition.

169245.explanation: For the necessary energy transition, it is important to investigate all alternatives for sustainable heat supply. Heat extraction from the Eem is one of the options. The municipality must actively explore feasible and affordable alternatives responsibly.

169246.explanation: Municipal policy touches on animal welfare in many areas, and we attach great importance to this. Therefore, we want to explicitly designate animal welfare as a theme within a councilor's portfolio. This way, this important topic receives attention and priority.

169247.explanation: The climate crisis threatens our future and that of our children. Generating energy from sun and wind is crucial to meeting our climate goals. Therefore, if there is a suitable location for wind turbines or solar fields in our municipality, we should cooperate. We avoid nature and meadow bird areas.

169249.explanation: GroenLinks stands for solidarity with people fleeing war and violence. The current shelter enables us to provide a home for many minor refugees. We see no alternative possibilities for smaller-scale shelter within our municipality.

169250.explanation: Of course, Baarn helps people fleeing war and violence. We show solidarity with other municipalities and comply with the distribution law. GroenLinks stands for permanent shelter and wants to keep the current asylum seekers' center as long as necessary.

169252.explanation: The library is an accessible place where everyone feels welcome. More educational activities, such as digital skills, meet the needs of Baarn residents. In this way, the library further develops into a broad cultural center that contributes to a lively Baarn.

169253.explanation: Our dreamed cultural center in the city center with all cultural facilities together unfortunately proved unfeasible. So we are looking for alternatives to maintain a diverse and accessible cultural offer. De Speeldoos must certainly remain part of this, due to its important role in culture and meeting nearby.

169254.explanation: The municipality must help our residents prepare for a crisis, in line with national policy. This can be done with an emergency placemat but also in other ways. The important thing is that we invest in a resilient society where people look out for each other locally.

169255.explanation: Camera surveillance is an infringement on our privacy. The municipality should therefore only use it in case of emergency. We prefer to invest in preventing criminal behavior rather than combating it.

169257.explanation: We consider sufficient deployment of enforcement officers and other municipal staff for safety and livability important. This applies both to enforcement on the street and behind the scenes. The deployment of extra enforcement officers requires careful consideration and is part of a broader approach.

169258.explanation: Young people need their own space. By ensuring good 'hangout spots' as a municipality, we take young people seriously and reduce nuisance. It is important that these places are set up in consultation and that, in case of nuisance, conversations are held with both young people and local residents.

169259.explanation: Fireworks cause nuisance to people, animals, and nature and can lead to dangerous situations.

169260.explanation: To address the housing shortage, we must utilize all possibilities. Converting vacant offices into homes does not take up extra space and is more sustainable than new construction. Municipal subsidies and advice help office owners to use vacancy for housing.

169263.explanation: For many low-income housing seekers, social rental housing is the only option to live independently. With at least 40% social rental housing, we better match the demand in Baarn. We also find it important to provide mid-rental homes for people who currently fall between the cracks.

169265.explanation: Financial support for school supplies, sports, and culture ensures that residents with low incomes can participate. This contributes to better health, valuable social contacts, and more enjoyment of life. This municipal support should complement national schemes.

169266.explanation: PIT is indispensable for residents dealing with poverty or debts. For people lost in the maze of regulations, one accessible desk is essential. Working more efficiently may be possible, but cuts must not come at the expense of support for vulnerable residents.

169267.explanation: It is important that the municipality commits to people who find it difficult to get work. There is already a well-functioning regional provision: RWA-Amfors. But the municipality also has a role model function in offering opportunities to people distant from the labor market.

169268.explanation: Residents with financial worries benefit from quick help close to home. This does not necessarily have to be via a physical location in every neighborhood, but it must be through recognizable and low-threshold access to caregivers. PIT Baarn, with clear connections to the neighborhoods, can play an important role in this.

169269.explanation: Paid parking can contribute to addressing local and/or temporary nuisance in rural areas or some neighborhoods. GroenLinks finds it reasonable to also ask users of parking spaces in the center for a modest contribution, as they use public space.

169271.explanation: We can make the center safer and livelier by banning cars. However, we must consider the effects on traffic flows in surrounding streets. Therefore, an integrated center traffic plan is necessary.

169272.explanation: Stimulating shared transport is a good idea. The municipality can support and make carsharing more attractive by designating (free) parking spaces for shared cars.

169273.explanation: More of these spots will encourage electric driving, which makes an important contribution to limiting CO2 emissions. However, electric cars are not affordable for everyone. It is important that the number of charging stations increases proportionally with the growth of electric cars.

169274.explanation: If the expansion of parking permits proves necessary, for example due to nuisance, this can be done in consultation with the neighborhood if there is support. We cherish our public space, and paying for parking is simply a usage fee for that space.

169275.explanation: Public space in Baarn is still much too designed for car use. By giving pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport more space, we make the municipality healthier and more livable. Walking and cycling contribute to cleaner air and safer traffic.

169276.explanation: Public places to meet strengthen community spirit and reduce loneliness. We don’t have to reinvent the wheel; many neighborhoods already have such places that we can further encourage and promote. It is very important, for example, that Lokaal-O is preserved.

169278.explanation: It is important to keep costs manageable, but not at the expense of young people who need care. Often expensive care can be prevented with prevention and better cooperation. The municipality must also lobby together with other municipalities for sufficient funding from the national government.

169279.explanation: There is little to do for young people in Baarn. This can make them feel that the municipality is not there for them. By organizing more activities in consultation with young people, they will feel seen and involved.

169284.explanation: Neighborhood residents who want to help must be given all the space and opportunities to do so. Encouraging voluntary help with maintenance is a good idea. The municipality can do much more together with residents. They know best what they want in their neighborhood.

169285.explanation: Neighborhood coaches can play an important role in improving livability in the districts. They provide low-threshold help for questions about care and welfare and can support residents with neighborhood initiatives.

169287.explanation: For every housing seeker, it is hard when you cannot move forward. In general, we must create more and more suitable housing. Prioritizing local housing seekers can also help keep young people in Baarn and encourage the flow of people leaving a small home.

169288.explanation: Good public transport is important for various reasons: to participate, for the environment, and for the accessibility of the centers. By investing in tailor-made public transport, the municipality ensures better accessibility, more independence, and less dependence on the car.

169229.explanation: We do find it important to strive for equal pay for all people and an equal distribution of power positions, but we do not want to make it mandatory. We assume the best people in the right position.

169230.explanation: We do what we can ourselves. When necessary, it remains possible to ask for external advice. Sometimes, especially in a small municipality, it is necessary to involve a specialist because we do not have all the knowledge in-house.

169231.explanation: We find it important that citizens can make their voices heard. Sometimes a referendum is the right tool for that. There are also other ways to involve citizens, such as an information evening.

169232.explanation: Last year, a plan was adopted by the council to improve sports facilities at De Geerenweg. This plan is widely supported by sports clubs. D66 Baarn wholeheartedly supports this plan.

169233.explanation: All young people in Baarn have the right to sports and culture. There are already many opportunities for young people to participate, including a fund for sports and culture for all residents who need it. D66 Baarn continues to support this.

169234.explanation: We support a programmed theater in Baarn, but it does not necessarily have to be at the current location.

169235.explanation: The money in the municipality’s savings fund does not have to remain fixed; it is the money of all residents. For a major project or a sustainable investment, it may be used for the benefit of Baarn’s residents.

169237.explanation: D66 encourages organizing events to keep Baarn lively. Costs are already charged for this, and these must at least be cost-covering.

169238.explanation: There are various types of subsidies, such as subsidies for sustainability, social organizations, or events. D66 is positive about this and finds it important that the money is well spent. Therefore, we assess each application individually and regularly review the subsidy amounts.

169239.explanation: D66 wants a vibrant center. Therefore, we stimulate the organization of events in the center. For this, we have a cultural coach and a subsidy scheme.

169240.explanation: D66 wants Baarn to be attractive to tourists. Think, for example, of Baarn’s gems such as Groeneveld, Palace Soestdijk, the center, and Lage Vuursche. With tourism, we stimulate the local economy.

169241.explanation: The climate crisis is urgent. Action is needed worldwide, in the Netherlands, and locally. D66 wants Baarn to be as climate neutral as possible by 2030. We aim for 55% less CO₂ in 2030 and a climate-neutral Baarn by 2050, together with residents and businesses.

169245.explanation: D66 wants to explore and support all possibilities to generate sustainable energy, with heat recovery from the Eem as one example.

169246.explanation: Animal welfare is important and is sufficiently covered in the current responsibilities of the aldermen.

169247.explanation: D66 wants to explore and support all possibilities to generate sustainable energy; this includes wind turbines and solar panels.

169249.explanation: As a municipality, we have a legal obligation to have sufficient shelter places in the municipality. D66 believes that genuine refugees should be accommodated. The current location is the best for this.

169250.explanation: As a municipality, we have a legal obligation to have sufficient shelter places in the municipality. D66 finds it important to take our responsibility for this.

169252.explanation: Educational activities are already organized in the library. The library is a central place in Baarn that is well suited for this purpose.

169253.explanation: We are in favor of a programmed theater in Baarn. Preferably, there will be a cultural center together with the library. This does not mean that the Speeldoos must close. A theater contributes to a vibrant center.

169254.explanation: D66 considers it important that all residents are well informed so that quick action can be taken in case of emergency.

169255.explanation: D66 Baarn regards safety on the streets as very important. In exceptional cases, camera surveillance can contribute to this. However, it must always be considered whether it is useful.

169257.explanation: We value safety. D66 believes the cause of a problem must always be examined. Enforcement is one possible solution. Currently, there are sufficient enforcement officers employed by the municipality of Baarn.

169258.explanation: D66 thinks it is important that young people have a pleasant place. The council has approved the creation of a place for youth in the Pekingtuin.

169259.explanation: D66 considers it important that New Year's Eve is pleasant and safe for everyone.

169260.explanation: We want to manage vacancy smartly. Converting offices into homes is part of this. The municipality can support this, for example by issuing permits. Providing subsidies is not part of this support.

169263.explanation: D66 Baarn supports the municipality’s established policy, which states that the guideline is 30%.

169265.explanation: We find it important that everyone can participate. Therefore, people with little money should receive financial support from the municipality. Baarn already offers more financial support than other municipalities in the Netherlands. D66 wants to maintain this.

169266.explanation: D66 supports the budget cuts already implemented at PIT Baarn; further cuts are considered irresponsible.

169267.explanation: D66 Baarn wants everyone to be able to participate.

169268.explanation: Baarn already has good and accessible support for people with financial worries. Active contact is sought with them. PIT Baarn plays a major role in this. According to D66, this access is especially important in neighborhoods where financial worries occur more frequently.

169269.explanation: Currently, free parking still works well enough; in the future, a parking permit might be a solution to parking pressure in neighborhoods.

169271.explanation: Given the current parking problems, we do not foresee this happening soon. In the future, for example, a parking garage could be a way to make the center car-free.

169272.explanation: We welcome car sharing. However, it is not the municipality’s task to finance car sharing.

169273.explanation: There must be sufficient charging points for electric cars; we want to achieve this by creating fast chargers on the outskirts of Baarn and at gas stations.

169274.explanation: Currently, free parking still works well enough; in the future, a parking permit might be a solution to parking pressure in neighborhoods.

169275.explanation: D66 Baarn wants to encourage residents to leave their car at home. To achieve this, roads must be friendly for pedestrians and cyclists, and public transport must be accessible.

169276.explanation: D66 Baarn supports meeting places close to every resident.

169278.explanation: The municipality is legally and morally obliged to provide care to every child that needs help.

169279.explanation: The young people in Baarn have been saying for some time that more should be organized for them. We are happy to respond to this.

169284.explanation: When initiatives arise in a neighborhood to maintain parts of that area themselves, this should be possible and supported by the municipality. It should not become an obligation to maintain your own neighborhood.

169285.explanation: D66 Baarn wants to deploy residents from the neighborhood as neighborhood coaches who maintain close contact with the municipality and fellow residents.

169287.explanation: Everyone must get equal opportunities for housing, based on registration duration and any urgency.

169288.explanation: Good public transport encourages residents to leave their cars at home.

169229.explanation: We actively strive for a balanced male-female ratio because good representation leads to better decisions. However, we do not enforce this in advance; the quality and availability of candidates determine a knowledgeable and strong executive board.

169230.explanation: We build on the strengths of our own civil servants and residents. Fewer expensive reports mean more money for the neighborhood. We strengthen our own organization and only hire specialists when that expertise is truly needed. This way, we spend public money smartly and socially.

169231.explanation: The municipal council is elected to decide. But we like to listen. It is important that you think along about plans in your neighborhood, so they truly meet your needs. We choose good pre-discussion rather than a referendum. Ultimately, the council makes the decisions.

169232.explanation: Baarn already has many good and accessible sports facilities. Expansion is not necessary, but proper maintenance is. By organizing the sports park more efficiently along the Geerenweg, we create space for housing. We opt for affordable homes for starters and seniors. This way, sport remains enjoyable and we can build.

169233.explanation: Every child must be able to participate. The Youth Fund for Sport & Culture already works well. Thanks to us, swimming lessons are now reimbursed for those who cannot pay themselves. We closely monitor the budget: if needed, we allocate additional funds. Sport and culture are indispensable for a good start.

169234.explanation: Culture is indispensable. Therefore, we will maintain the library and theater. We are investing 14 million euros to renew De Speeldoos on Rembrandtlaan into a broad cultural center. This guarantees a beautiful venue for performing arts and social gatherings in our village.

169235.explanation: The general reserve is not a piggy bank for show, but money belonging to all of us. We do not hoard it, but invest it wisely in your wellbeing and our facilities. Expected extra income is immediately used to help Baarn move forward. In this way, we build a strong future.

169237.explanation: Organizers already pay costs for their permits. We do not want extra taxes that hinder events because they bring atmosphere and energy to our village. It is more important that local retailers in the center benefit from these visitors than that the municipality makes extra money from this.

169238.explanation: Subsidies are indispensable for our associations and care. Cutting them is not an option. However, we critically assess whether they work. We make choices: initiatives that work well receive extra support; less effective subsidies are stopped. Every euro must be well spent.

169239.explanation: A vibrant center is good for the atmosphere. We want a lively Brink, but the municipality does not have to organize everything itself. We prefer to support entrepreneurs and residents who organize events, for example with subsidies. This way, we facilitate conviviality, but the initiative lies with residents.

169240.explanation: More tourists is not an end in itself. We choose quality and spreading out crowds. We encourage visitors to leave their car behind. Baarn station is the ideal gateway to nature: from there, you can continue on foot or with a shared bike. This keeps the forest quiet and our village accessible.

169241.explanation: We want to speed up sustainability, but due to the congested power grid, 2035 is challenging. More important is that everyone can afford it. We will not proceed faster than residents can financially handle. We support those with difficulties, so support remains. Everyone must be able to participate.

169245.explanation: We must get off natural gas. That is why we are looking for a joint heating solution. This could be heat from the Eem (aquathermy), but also from the sewage or waste. We will not focus on one source but broadly explore all clean options for a sustainable future.

169246.explanation: Animal welfare is important, but no separate alderman is needed for it. It must be a fixed part of the policy of the alderman for public space.

169247.explanation: We cannot only point fingers at others; Baarn must also generate clean energy. We will start the discussion about solar and wind energy. Our demands are strict: it must fit into the landscape and be 100% locally owned. This way, the profit goes to residents, not investors.

169249.explanation: The Distribution Act remains, so we keep open the shelter for 152 refugees. If we can also make this location suitable for our own residents, we see that as a great opportunity. This way, everyone is offered housing and we prevent groups from displacing each other.

169250.explanation: The Distribution Act remains, so we keep open the shelter for 152 refugees. If we can also make this location suitable for our own residents, we see that as a great opportunity. This way, everyone is offered housing and we prevent groups from displacing each other.

169252.explanation: The library is the living room of the village and a place for development. Language courses, digital skills, and debates belong there. Knowledge must be accessible to everyone.

169253.explanation: Culture is indispensable. Therefore, we will maintain the library and theater. We are investing 14 million euros to renew De Speeldoos on Rembrandtlaan into a broad cultural center. This guarantees a beautiful venue for performing arts and social gatherings in our village.

169254.explanation: We believe in 'collective strength.' Safety is created together. Rather than handing out placemats, we invest in strong neighborhoods where neighbors know and help each other in emergencies. The municipality supports this network and, of course, provides quick, clear information when needed.

169255.explanation: Cameras are an absolute last resort. We believe more in real eyes and ears on the street. That's why we opt for visible neighborhood police officers and extra community enforcement officers. They know the neighborhood and solve problems immediately. This works better and is nicer for your privacy.

169257.explanation: Community enforcement officers can't solve everything; we remain responsible for our own behavior. Still, extra supervision is necessary. Therefore, we will hire two additional neighborhood community enforcement officers to enhance safety in the district. Anyone breaking the rules will receive an immediate fine. We no longer give warnings.

169258.explanation: Young people need space. We provide safe meeting places where they are not sent away. We do this together with the youth so it truly becomes their place. This prevents nuisance in the neighborhood and gives young people the space they deserve.

169259.explanation: Community enforcement officers alone cannot stop fireworks nuisance. We support the national ban starting in 2026. Instead, we want to celebrate New Year's Eve together with a central show, for example using drones. This is festive, safe, and brings all of Baarn together.

169260.explanation: Housing is a right, not a market. Converting vacant offices is a quick and sustainable way to create homes. We do not provide subsidies but see great opportunities here for the housing corporation Omthuis. This way, we help starters and seniors find housing and turn empty places into pleasant homes again.

169263.explanation: Housing is a right, not a market. We demand 30% social rental housing and 40% mid-range housing in new developments. This allows people with middle incomes to finally move into suitable homes. This frees up social rental homes for those who need them most. Together we keep Baarn affordable.

169265.explanation: No one may be left out. We have ensured new policies based on subsistence security. We increase budgets for school supplies, sports, and culture. Especially in difficult times, participation is crucial. Every child in Baarn deserves a fair chance and a good start.

169266.explanation: PIT Baarn does indispensable work for residents with concerns. Cutting budgets is not an end in itself, but we do assess whether it can be done more efficiently. The most important thing is: funding matches the tasks. They receive the money needed to help people well. This keeps support always nearby and accessible.

169267.explanation: Work gives appreciation. We transform the sheltered workplace into a broad development company. 'Broad' means we help everyone who finds work challenging with tailored coaching and training. We focus on what you can do so that everyone gets a fair chance at a suitable and pleasant workplace.

169268.explanation: Help with financial worries must always be nearby. We want neighborhood teams visibly present in your own area. This lowers the threshold to seek help. By intervening early, we prevent debts from growing. We come to you so that no one faces problems alone.

169269.explanation: The blue zone in the center remains free. We regulate parking pressure with permits, not paid parking. Only at estates in the rural area do we see paid parking as an opportunity to finance nature maintenance. This keeps Baarn accessible and preserves the greenery.

169271.explanation: The Brink should be the vibrant heart of Baarn. We will investigate whether the part near the horeca can be made car-free on weekends. This creates space for larger terraces and pleasant meetings. This makes our center safer, cosier, and more attractive for all visitors.

169272.explanation: We want to create hubs with shared cars and cargo bikes in every neighborhood. Shared transport saves you money and creates more space on the street. This way, parking spots become places for greenery or playing children again. We gladly support residents who want to organize this together.

169273.explanation: The future is electric, but space in Baarn is limited. Because we already have a good network, we install new charging stations only where really necessary. Our focus is primarily on shared mobility and central hubs. This keeps the street livable and makes sustainable driving possible for everyone.

169274.explanation: Parking permits are a tool to distribute scarce spots fairly. We implement them only if parking pressure is too high and residents request it. This way, those who really need their car can park close to home. It is not an end in itself but a way to keep neighborhoods livable.

169275.explanation: We choose safety and health on the streets. Using the STOMP method, we prioritize pedestrians and cyclists, followed by good public transport. This makes Baarn more accessible, greener, and safer for everyone, from young to old.

169276.explanation: We tackle loneliness together. With a 'neighborhood budget,' we empower residents to create their own meeting places. Whether it is a coffee morning or another activity: you know best what your neighborhood needs. Together, we build collective strength throughout Baarn.

169278.explanation: The care demand determines the budget, not the other way around. Our principle is: help as light as possible and as heavy as necessary. We invest an extra 200,000 euros in prevention to intervene early. This prevents serious problems and ensures no child is left out in the cold. Every child deserves appropriate care.

169279.explanation: Young people need their own place. We support youth work and create safe meeting spots (JOPs) together. With an active youth council, young people get to co-decide what happens in Baarn. This ensures offers that truly suit them.

169284.explanation: Gardening together is good for social cohesion but should never replace professionals to cut costs. Quality comes first. Through a 'neighborhood budget,' we give residents real influence and resources to beautify their own living environment.

169285.explanation: We choose neighborhood coaches as connectors, alongside neighborhood teams and community enforcement officers. They link residents, signal problems early, and stimulate initiatives. An indispensable social investment that directly improves the livability and strength of our neighborhoods.

169287.explanation: Baarn's youth and elderly should be able to continue living in their own village. Local ties must weigh more heavily in allocation. We fully utilize the Housing Act for local priority. Precisely because space is scarce, we must first offer housing to our own residents.

169288.explanation: Accessibility is crucial but requires smart customization. We focus specifically on Lage Vuursche to reduce recreational pressure through hubs and shared mobility (bicycles). This is more efficient than traditional public transport and keeps our centers accessible without cars dominating.

169229.explanation: It is positive if a mixed executive board is formed. The last two female aldermen in Baarn were from the CDA. However, making this mandatory goes too far.

169230.explanation: The executive delays decisions and often commissions extra studies by external agencies. Example: the Wilhelminavijver. Instead of repairing the leak, plans and studies for redesign were developed. The CDA wants fewer studies and more execution.

169231.explanation: Referendums reduce complex decisions to a simple yes/no choice, which does not do justice to the content. They increase divisions instead of solutions. In municipal elections, residents choose representatives who make well-considered decisions on their behalf.

169232.explanation: CDA Baarn stands for rapid implementation of the future vision for sports facilities at Geerenweg 2025-2040. Space for more sports is an integral part of that.

169233.explanation: CDA Baarn supports continuing the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture, making it possible for young people from low-income families to participate affordably in sports, thus keeping sports accessible for all youth.

169234.explanation: The CDA wants an affordable and accessible Culture House in the center: a place for film, theater, library, and meeting. Suitable for Baarn's scale. The Speeldoos costs over €600,000 in subsidies annually, which is financially unsustainable. Housing can be developed at that location.

169235.explanation: We must prevent debts from increasing further. The general reserve, our savings, has become significantly smaller under this administration due to frequent use. If this pace continues, there will be no reserve left in a few years. Large expenses must be saved for in advance.

169237.explanation: Many events are organized in Baarn. This is good for Baarn's vibrancy. To reduce municipal costs, it is reasonable to require a contribution from events with a profit motive.

169238.explanation: The CDA does not want to cut subsidies for associations and events, but policies must be more efficient with clear choices and frameworks. This ensures effective use of resources without losing activities like King’s Day.

169239.explanation: The municipality must properly facilitate associations and organizations in organizing events in the center, for example with a central contact point and clear permit information. However, we do not consider large subsidies justifiable if cuts are needed elsewhere.

169240.explanation: More tourists in Baarn are welcome, but given the current financial position, increasing investment in marketing Baarn as a tourist destination is not a priority. Entrepreneurs should also actively contribute to this themselves.

169241.explanation: CDA Baarn supports the plans in the Regional Energy Strategy. The goal is climate neutrality by 2050. Accelerating this to 2035 is unrealistic and too expensive.

169245.explanation: Heat extraction from the Eem seems promising and deserves research. However, we are concerned about affordability. For the CDA, it is important that sustainable energy remains affordable for residents. If research shows this is not feasible, the CDA does not want to impose this option on its residents.

169246.explanation: Animal welfare is, according to CDA Baarn, part of the areas of nature and environment and public space management. Therefore, we do not see added value in a separate alderman for animal welfare.

169247.explanation: The CDA chooses measures that fit Baarn’s green character. No large solar fields or windmills, but solar panels on roofs and small-scale projects with residents. Small solar fields along the A1 or a small wind turbine at farmers are acceptable, provided they are well integrated into the landscape.

169249.explanation: We take our responsibility for the national task of housing refugees based on the allocation numbers from the Distribution Act. The current asylum seeker center is necessary in this context and the current location in the forest near the Palace is a good spot.

169250.explanation: We take our responsibility for the national task of housing refugees based on the mandatory numbers in the Distribution Act.

169252.explanation: A modern library should also provide space for educational activities.

169253.explanation: The CDA wants an affordable and accessible Culture House in the center: a lively hub for film, library, a modest theater, and social gatherings. The Speeldoos currently receives over €600,000 in subsidies per year, which is financially unsustainable. That location could then be used for housing.

169254.explanation: Placemats in a kitchen drawer don't help; there is a need for practical, immediately available information.

169255.explanation: We think increased surveillance through cameras is a good idea and a better alternative than deploying more community enforcement officers (BOAs).

169257.explanation: We prefer to focus on encouraging people to take responsibility for their own living environment and, where possible, use camera surveillance. Additional BOAs involve significant costs.

169258.explanation: Baarn already has several 'hangout spots.' These are only moderately visited. Young people choose their own places. Community life and sports are helpful. Nuisance requires enforcement (police and BOAs).

169259.explanation: Strict enforcement where necessary. Addressing parents of troublemakers is obviously also important.

169260.explanation: The CDA supports transforming (vacant) offices into housing. This fits the urgent need for more (affordable) homes. However, municipal subsidies are not needed for this.

169263.explanation: Municipal policy in Baarn requires that at least 30% of new construction is social rental housing. A higher percentage is financially unfeasible for developers. Such a high percentage would also lead to less new construction.

169265.explanation: This is already well arranged in Baarn through the schools, the Youth Sports & Culture Fund, and the Adult Sports & Culture Fund.

169266.explanation: Money worries are often the root of other problems. Cutting help for poverty and debt is false economy, which you will eventually pay for. However, the agreed cuts to PIT must be found elsewhere.

169267.explanation: This is already well organized at the regional level with the Regional Social Employment Agency Amersfoort and Surroundings (Amfors), which we jointly organize with other municipalities.

169268.explanation: This does not need to be arranged per neighborhood. It can simply be handled via the town hall/PIT.

169269.explanation: Paying does not solve anything. Free parking makes Baarn extra attractive to visitors. This keeps the center lively and ensures our shops get enough customers.

169271.explanation: A car-free Brink would lead to much bigger traffic problems elsewhere. Think, for example, of Dalweg and Kerkstraat, with their schools.

169272.explanation: The municipality can facilitate this with, for example, charging stations or shared car spots, but no financial support.

169273.explanation: Baarn has significantly fewer public charging points than other (comparable) municipalities and there are more and more hybrid and electric cars. Not everyone has the option to charge their car on private property.

169274.explanation: Parking permits do not solve anything. Owning a car is in many cases not a choice but a necessity.

169275.explanation: We want to locally reduce car traffic by improving and expanding walking and cycling paths, and more 30 km/h zones and shared streets. This is good for safety, livability, health, and the environment.

169276.explanation: Social cohesion is very important. Neighbors should be able to meet and support each other. Besides existing 'neighborhood rooms,' such as in Oosterkwartier and Lokaal O, these can also be occasionally available spaces. For example, places that sometimes serve as polling stations.

169278.explanation: Costs for youth care are spiraling out of control. Family, education, and volunteers can be more involved to solve problems together. Group youth care is often efficient and effective. This way, we keep expensive youth care available for young people with more serious issues.

169279.explanation: Not for young people, but with young people. This increases their involvement and shared responsibility.

169284.explanation: This often works well and ensures social cohesion in the neighborhood. Of course, on a voluntary basis. This does not relieve the municipality from the duty to provide good care for these locations.

169285.explanation: Preferably increase neighborhood cohesion, for example with neighborhood rooms, and enforcement of nuisance by the community officer. An additional civil servant for this is not the solution.

169287.explanation: CDA Baarn wants to use all the space that laws and regulations allow.

169288.explanation: This is the responsibility of the province. The municipality must of course continue to urge the province for good accessibility of the town centers. The Senior Bus is important and will remain.

169229.explanation: What matters most to us is the quality of the individual. It's high time we have another female alderman.

169230.explanation: We believe there is sufficient wisdom, experience, and knowledge among our residents. In our view, everything has already been researched.

169231.explanation: In our opinion, it is unnecessary.

169232.explanation: Good for vitality, creates a connection between young and old. This is already included in the sports vision.

169233.explanation: There are already arrangements for young people who cannot afford it. We do support a social buffer for anyone who can use it.

169234.explanation: For us, the only right place in Baarn. Renewal could be spread over several years. The challenge is to keep renewal affordable.

169235.explanation: The current rules for the general reserve are adhered to. We do not agree with the opinion that the municipality is squandering funds.

169237.explanation: For commercial events this can be considered; for local events such as King's Day, All-together, and the Sinterklaas parade, certainly not.

169238.explanation: Subsidies are not an expense but an investment in young and old and in the livability of Baarn. 50Plus Baarn believes that 60% of OZB revenues should be allocated to subsidies. That does not mean "it has to be spent." What remains can benefit a local social fund to tackle stark inequality of opportunity.

169239.explanation: Much is already organized to keep the center lively and that is solid. External communication can be improved.

169240.explanation: This puts Baarn on the national map, allowing entrepreneurs to profit as well. Let's also ensure more overnight accommodation options.

169241.explanation: Greenhouse gases must be reduced as quickly as possible; a deadline helps with that. Many other factors also contribute to climate warming, which we as a municipality partly cannot influence.

169245.explanation: A district heating network based on heat extraction from the Eem must first be demonstrably a truly good plan; what are the constructional and financial consequences for the residents involved?

169246.explanation: Animal welfare certainly deserves the necessary attention, but a separate alderman for this is overkill.

169247.explanation: Baarn has limited options. Blind cooperation leads to lawsuits, but too much obstruction leads to a strained relationship with the province and other municipalities.

169249.explanation: The COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers) has far too few employees here.

169250.explanation: In accordance with the Dispersion Act and with maximum influence from the municipality of Baarn.

169252.explanation: Approach to digital illiteracy, lifelong development, reading skills among youth, language lessons for non-native speakers. This is a task for every library and the Baarn Library must excel in this as well.

169253.explanation: We want to renovate and keep programming the current theater, which is not possible in a smaller theater. There are already sufficient cultural venues (Nutsgebouw and Hoofdstraat 1).

169254.explanation: In itself fine. Other options are good as well, as long as they are clear for our residents.

169255.explanation: This should be seriously considered especially in nuisance hotspots.

169257.explanation: We hold the view that rules and laws are fine, as long as they are enforced.

169258.explanation: In the past we had the Boemerang. A location like this could be a solution.

169259.explanation: This is definitely necessary. Nuisance must be tackled more firmly through collaboration between community officers, police, and mayor.

169260.explanation: We are certainly in favour of new homes. But we wonder with what subsidy this would have to be done.

169263.explanation: We would prefer to raise the percentage even further. Onwards to 70%!

169265.explanation: There are already various schemes; let's ensure they are well utilized. Support can be provided if people demonstrably need it.

169266.explanation: Do not cut further here unless alternative financing is possible. Continuity is needed to keep sailing a steady course.

169267.explanation: Let's leave this ball with Eemkracht.

169268.explanation: We want even more attention for a neighborhood-focused approach.

169269.explanation: Our inhabitant is not a cash cow!

169271.explanation: It would be very nice but how to realize this? It will have a great impact on traffic circulation.

169272.explanation: A financial incentive is not the solution when sharing cars.

169273.explanation: This must keep pace with the number of electric cars on public roads.

169275.explanation: We would like to go a step further and offer free public transport to residents aged 65 or older.

169276.explanation: We prefer an annual neighborhood consultation with municipality and residents, and a neighborhood coach who stimulates initiatives per neighborhood. A community living room must be set up and run by and for residents themselves.

169278.explanation: We have a €1.7 million shortfall in youth care and that deficit must be reduced to zero. This can only be achieved through prevention.

169279.explanation: Youth is already on the agenda in Baarn and this is being included. Youth is actively involved in this.

169284.explanation: The municipality must collaborate well with neighborhoods that come up with good initiatives.

169285.explanation: Neighborhood coaches/village supporters have proven effective in strengthening neighborhoods and neighborhood initiatives.

169287.explanation: However, there are multiple criteria that may apply, such as economic ties.

169288.explanation: This must be arranged by the province.

169229.explanation: Ideally, the number of female and male members of the College should be equal. But we do not want to make this mandatory, because it also depends on the number of available people in a party. So we do not think an obligation is a good idea.

169230.explanation: Sometimes external research is useful and necessary. But often research is also used to delay or pass the hot potato. The municipality should rely more on its own strength and therefore may commission less external research.

169231.explanation: The municipality has invested heavily in public participation in recent years. Moreover, the municipal council always represents all residents of Baarn. A referendum adds nothing to this.

169232.explanation: Everyone should be able to play sports in Baarn. But that doesn't mean it all has to happen at one and the same location. Sports like table tennis, freerunning, and gymnastics each have a good dedicated location elsewhere.

169233.explanation: There are already good arrangements in Baarn for children from low-income families. If we also open these facilities for culture, we make it possible for all Baarn youth to engage in sports and cultural activities.

169234.explanation: It is high time to make this decision and give De Speeldoos the future it deserves. Only in this way do we keep a wonderful cultural meeting center in Baarn that can last for decades.

169235.explanation: Reserves exist to save up for worse times or unexpected events.

169237.explanation: It is high time that Baarn collects its own event tax, because people also make large-scale use of our local facilities at bigger events.

169238.explanation: It is good to look at the effectiveness of subsidies, but the municipality has just had a solid subsidy policy since last year. The municipality always has a role in enabling initiatives, but that doesn't have to be only about money.

169239.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality, but of the entrepreneurs in the center. Of course, the municipality can contribute ideas and help.

169240.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality, which already does more than enough to put Baarn on the tourist map together with others (TIP). Promising events can of course always be supported.

169241.explanation: Striving for this is admirable and important. At the same time, Baarn can never do this alone. We will always have to approach this realistically.

169245.explanation: Every sustainable energy source must be utilized optimally, including 'our' Eem river. Therefore, the municipality must take the lead in this in consultation with the province and the water board.

169246.explanation: Aldermen divide tasks and responsibilities among themselves. Animal welfare is always simply part of that.

169247.explanation: Although suitable locations in Baarn are not always easy to find, the municipality must commit to sustainable energy at appropriate locations within the municipality.

169249.explanation: Baarn must always maintain the ability to accommodate refugees.

169250.explanation: Baarn must always maintain the ability to accommodate refugees.

169252.explanation: The library has 5 statutory tasks and simply carries them out in close consultation with the municipality.

169253.explanation: This has been extensively researched and turned out not to be feasible. The Speeldoos must be renewed in its current location into a cultural center for the future.

169254.explanation: A lot is already done to raise awareness about resilience in times of crisis. That is good and important. The municipality should not add its own extra layer with its own resources.

169255.explanation: This must be considered on a case-by-case basis. It worked very well during the recent explosion wave. But camera surveillance is not a goal in itself.

169257.explanation: There is a need for more street supervision. Perhaps cooperation with our neighboring municipalities Soest and Bunschoten is an option for this.

169258.explanation: Young people deserve a nice place to come together in Baarn. Think of a fitness playground in the Pekingtuin.

169259.explanation: BOAs (public enforcement officers) may certainly act more strictly against vandalism and nuisance. However, since the national fireworks ban, fireworks are not a task for BOAs but for the police.

169260.explanation: This can only work well if it is absolutely certain that an office location is no longer suitable for offices in the long term.

169263.explanation: As the ChristianUnion has been saying for years: when it comes to the housing shortage, all taboos must go out the window. Affordable housing is crucial in this regard.

169265.explanation: This works best through the Social Participation Budget. Through this provision, you simplify arrangements for people and the municipality also operates from trust and people’s own strength.

169266.explanation: The municipality must ensure that PIT Baarn operates as effectively as possible for all residents of Baarn. If this costs money, the municipality may reserve the necessary funds for this.

169267.explanation: Baarn is simply too small for this alone. In cooperation with neighboring municipalities such as Amersfoort (as with Amfors), Baarn can keep low-threshold work available.

169268.explanation: A small-scale municipal support point in every neighborhood helps provide people with the assistance they need close to home. This is obviously not just about money, but all forms of support.

169269.explanation: Paid parking is not taboo, but it must not become a cash cow for the municipality. Moreover, its effects must be properly measured.

169271.explanation: Car-free would make Baarn’s center inaccessible. However, a bicycle street can be established - like on Vondellaan - to improve traffic safety.

169272.explanation: The municipality can take the initiative to offer car sharing, but direct financial support to residents is not necessary.

169273.explanation: The municipality must provide charging facilities, but this can also be done at designated parking lots or carpool sites.

169274.explanation: In busy neighborhoods, a parking permit may be necessary to reduce parking nuisance. But it must not become a cash cow for the municipality and it must be properly enforced. Clear new policy from the municipality is therefore needed first.

169276.explanation: This can also be a way to get in contact with the municipality easily, because municipal officials are regularly present in people’s living rooms.

169278.explanation: The municipality must primarily gain more insight into the costs. Based on that, the municipality should spend what is necessary to ensure that young people receive the help they deserve.

169279.explanation: The current work of the youth coordinator and youth workers is positive, so the municipality should definitely continue this.

169284.explanation: The municipality must facilitate and encourage neighborhood residents if they want to maintain the public space in their environment themselves. They may also be rewarded for this.

169285.explanation: Separate neighborhood coaches are not necessary, but with local community living rooms we can improve livability and social cohesion in Baarn neighborhoods.

169287.explanation: However, this is not a task for the municipality but for better legislation from The Hague.

169288.explanation: Public transport in Baarn is very important, but not a task for the municipality. The municipality can, however, strongly lobby the province on this.

statements.title: Theater de Speeldoos should be completely rebuilt on its current site.
statements.moreInfo: Theater de Speeldoos has been Baarn's cultural center since 1975. The building, with its characteristic "red plush" atmosphere, is however outdated and no longer meets current standards. This has led to discussions in the municipal council about renovation or new construction.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.
The municipality should use its reserve fund only for unexpected expenses.
By 2035 Baarn must emit no greenhouse gases so the municipality no longer contributes to global warming.
The residents of Baarn should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.
statements.moreInfo: In a referendum the municipality puts a single-issue question to voters, who can vote for or against. All residents of the municipality who are 18 or older may vote. Both the municipal council and citizens can request a referendum. The result is not binding, which means the municipality does not have to follow it.

statements.title: The municipality should provide fewer subsidies.
The municipality should cooperate with plans for wind turbines or fields of solar panels.
The municipality should expand the sports facilities along Geerenweg, providing space for all sports.

titleParts.glossary: Buildings or services needed for daily life, such as roads, sports facilities or healthcare.

titleParts.text: langs de Geerenweg uitbreiden, met ruimte voor alle sporten.

statements.title: At least 40% of the new homes built in Baarn must be social-rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social-rental.
statements.moreInfo: The current Housing Program of the municipality of Baarn states that in every building plan the share of social rental housing must be at least 30%.

statements.title: The municipality should ask for less advice from external research firms.
The practice of housing many asylum seekers in one location in our municipality should end as soon as possible.
The municipality must not cut funding for PIT Baarn, the service desk for help with poverty and debt.
statements.moreInfo: PIT Baarn is the broad welfare organization that works to ensure all residents can participate in the community - from parenting support to meeting activities for the elderly, and support for residents in poverty or with debts. In 2025 the municipality cut PIT's budget by €200,000.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV surveillance in Baarn.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should work to attract more tourists to Baarn and the surrounding area.
Baarn should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) to enforce safety, waste, environmental rules, and fireworks regulations.

titleParts.glossary: Someone who works for the municipality and ensures everyone follows the rules.

titleParts.text: s (boa’s) komen die gaan handhaven op veiligheid, afval, milieu en vuurwerk.

statements.title: Residents who want to share cars should receive financial support from the municipality.
The municipality should open a neighborhood community room in every area where residents can have coffee or take part in activities.
The municipality should open a modern sheltered workshop for everyone who has difficulty finding work.
Parking in Baarn should remain free.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
Organizers of events with more than one thousand visitors should pay a tax to cover the municipality's costs.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

titleParts.text: organiseert met meer dan duizend bezoekers, moet daarover belasting gaan betalen om de kosten voor de gemeente te dekken.

statements.title: The municipality must not spend more money on youth care.

titleParts.text: De gemeente mag niet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.moreInfo: Since 2015 the municipality of Baarn has been responsible for youth care. For that reason it has engaged youth support from the Local Team and specialist youth care from MetMaya. Partly because more young people suffer from issues such as stress, mental health problems or loneliness, the costs the municipality pays for youth care have risen sharply.

statements.title: More educational activities should be organized in the library.
The Brink in Baarn town center should become car-free.
The municipality should use neighborhood coaches to try to improve livability in the neighborhoods.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should use events to try to attract more people to the town center and the Brink.
The municipality should invest in tailored public transport between the town centers.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should invest in a special fund to give all young people access to sports and culture.
The municipality should distribute emergency placemats that explain what to do in a crisis.
The municipality should involve local residents in maintaining public spaces.
Baarn should introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.
Baarn should have an alderman responsible for animal welfare.
Baarn should continue to accommodate refugees.
The municipality should create a hangout for young people to reduce nuisance.
The municipality should organize more activities that appeal to young people.
Pedestrians, cyclists and public transport should be given more space than cars in Baarn.
Theater de Speeldoos should close to make way for a cultural center with a small theater in the town center.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should take tougher action against fireworks disturbances.
Residents with financial worries should be able to easily access help in every neighborhood.

shootoutStatements.theme: Heat extraction from the Eem River
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should do its utmost to extract heat from the Eem.
Low-income residents should receive more financial support from the municipality for school supplies, sports and culture.
